reviews.review_description: This game is so good. There are a variety of activities that for me, kept me there for like 10 minutes and were super fun, I didn’t get to experience the 2 other maps, mountain and jungle I think because my headset ran out of battery. I didn’t like job simulator as much as this because it had a boring vibe to it, but it was still a bit fun. I experienced no problems with this game at all, and I think it is very good and I definitely recommend.

reviews.review_title: Read this comment before you buy
reviews.review_description: So I was just like on my VR looking at different games and then I came across this one and I’m like oh this looks like a fun game and then I went and did all the tutorials and when I went to this map it makes you have to like do stuff like it makes you and it’s not that fun because like you have to wait and I went to the beach map, thinking it was like how it looks in the video. No it does not look like that and it’s not fun at all and then when you go to different maps only map that lets you like make stuff is mountains so please the developers of this game please make this a better game and actually allow us to make stuff in every map and I would like to get my money back

reviews.review_title: Lots of features for the price
reviews.review_description:
There's so much that you can do in here! I some of the things I love are: the use of problem solving and strategy, using items from different areas to to accomplish stuff in other areas, the customizablity of some parts, and even just relaxing in some areas... and of course throwing random stuff at anyone.

I already liked playing Job Simulator, but I wasn't expecting how much I like this spinoff/sequel. I'm also surprised that it only costs this much for such a good game. 

I'm kinda keeping this review mostly vague because I don't want to spoil it for anyone.

Thank you so much for this amazing game!
every single time i loaded in the game the first time i got it i was fine but now it's just it's just making me stuck on the ground good game thow

The best game I've played in VR. The beach is so much fun and so are the mountains and the snow. It's funny that you play with robots and if you collect 30 challenges, the robots have a party and it really feels like a real vacation.

The maps are pretty tiny for the price and in the work mode its so annoying to get plags every second plus when you reach e.x 100 200 300 level its the same excuse. I always stop playing when it happens its so predictable and boring. Anyways its pretty much a mid game but not worth the price.

And the other thing I have is the one on the right is a picture from my phone and I have a picture of the other two and the but I don’t know if I can get it to you or not but I can get it from you if you want to send it to me

developer_response.body: We're so happy you've been having a relaxing simulated vacation. Happy memory capturing!

reviews.review_description: Geil

**EDIT**

I saw the fix that the developer posted on another review and switching the boundary setting fixed it. Thank you!

**End edit**

I’ve had Vacation Simulator for about six months, and initially, it was a great experience—fun, well-polished, and creative. However, more recently, the game started spawning me in a random location in my playspace. Unfortunately, that location ends up being on top of my TV stand, making the game unplayable. I’ve noticed other users reporting similar issues. The same problem is now happening with Job Simulator as well. Recalibrating the screen doesn't help.

It seems like something in a recent update may have affected how the game handles player positioning or boundary detection. I’d love to see a patch or a manual recentering feature added so players can recalibrate their play area as needed. I really enjoy these games and hope to be able to play them again soon.

Thanks for making such fun VR experiences—just hoping for a fix to bring them back to life!

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you're back to the best vacation ever!

developer_response.body: If you're having boundary issues, try this! Access the Boundary Settings (Settings > Physical Space > Boundary > Create New Boundary) and choose your own boundary > Roomscale > Select a similar-sized area around you. There's a video with these steps on our Trello board: https://trello.com/c/8K1jf6QL/55-help-im-having-boundary-issues  Let us know if you need further help in our Discord (discord.gg/owlchemylabs)

As many other reviewers have mentioned, this game is currently unplayable, due to it forcing you to remain in a (seemingly random) spot that it has determined, which in my case, is inside furniture. Resetting the view doesn’t help. Recreating the room boundary doesn’t my help. Rebooting and launching from a better location doesn’t help.

This game will not stop insisting I stand somewhere I cannot in order to launch the game, and so, I cannot play.

(The same problem exists with Job Simulator)

developer_response.body: Access the Boundary Settings (Settings > Physical Space > Boundary > Create New Boundary) and choose your own boundary > Roomscale > Select a similar-sized area around you. If you need more help, please email us at owlchemysupport@google.com

reviews.review_description: This game is fantastic, and possibly one of the greatest sequals in gaming history, but there's just one problem with this game. developer_response.body: Glad to hear you're loving the game! Stuck with the boundary blues? Try re-setting up roomscale on whichever platform you are using and clearing your boundary history before redrawing a new one. If you're still stuck, please email owlchemysupport@google.com with your headset info. We're here to help!
